place: East Northport CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: East Northport grew around a 19th‑century railroad stop that the Long Island Rail Road moved to Larkfield Road in 1873.
The town’s name comes from the original settlement of Larkfield, which sat north of the modern junction of Larkfield, Vernon Valley, and Laurel Roads.
The Northport‑East Northport Public Library houses a rich Local History Center with oral‑history audio files and a Tracks Through Time collection.
The library’s vertical files hold newspapers, brochures, and ephemera covering Northport, East Northport, Asharoken, and Eaton’s Neck.
East Northport sits just east of historic Northport Village, where diners, shops, and waterfront views blend with the hamlet’s own commercial spots.
